obligations.reason: The debug interface may provide a path to security-sensitive safe domain components without requiring debug unlock or authentication, violating access control.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug master (adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug authorization state (e.g., debug_unlock signal, authentication challenge completed) must gate access to safe domain bus transactions.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> adbg_tap_top -> adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module -> AXI/LINT bus -> fabric -> safe_domain bus slave
obligations.uncertainty: Need to check if debug transactions are routed to safe domain bus slaves and if any gating logic exists.

obligations.reason: The debug module's internal control registers (e.g., command register) might be written via JTAG without prior authentication, allowing an attacker to trigger debug operations.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug user (via TAP)
obligations.object: debug module command/control register (inside adbg_axi_module or adbg_or1k_module)
obligations.expected_guard: A lock bit or authentication state that prevents debug command execution until debug is unlocked.
obligations.candidate_path: TDI -> adbg_tap_top -> module_select_i -> adbg_axi_module internal register update logic

obligations.reason: A debug lock mechanism may be defeated if the lock bit can be cleared by a reset, power glitch, or test mode without proper authentication, leaving the system in an unprotected state.
obligations.subject: Reset signal or test mode entry
obligations.operation: clear lock bit or deassert lock signal
obligations.object: debug lock state register (e.g., in adbg_or1k_status_reg or top-level)
obligations.expected_guard: Lock bit must be sticky; only clearable by a specific unlock sequence or under authenticated debug, not by normal reset.
obligations.candidate_path: reset logic (trstn_i, rstn_i) clearing lock register; or test mode selection overriding lock.
obligations.uncertainty: Need to check if a lock register exists and its reset behavior.

obligations.reason: Debug master-initiated AXI/LINT transactions may bypass processor-side memory protection checks (e.g., PMP) because they originate from a different master with separate access rights, potentially accessing protected memory regions.
obligations.subject: Debug master (adbg_axi_module)
obligations.object: system memory/DMEM/IMEM that has PMP restrictions
obligations.expected_guard: Debug accesses should be subject to the same memory protection policies, or debug should be disabled when PMP enforces restrictions. Alternatively, a separate debug access control matrix should gate addresses.
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_axi_module -> AXI bus -> memory controller; compare against PMP checks for CPU accesses.
obligations.uncertainty: Need to see if the debug master has fixed AXI IDs that bypass bus fabric access controls.

obligations.reason: Internal registers or FIFOs that temporarily hold cryptographic keys or sensitive plaintext could be readable via the debug bus if not properly cleared, exposing secrets.
obligations.subject: Debug master
obligations.object: internal buffer/register holding secret material (e.g., in crypto wrapper)
obligations.expected_guard: Such data paths must not be mapped into debug address space, or must be zeroized after operation and before any debug read possibility.
obligations.candidate_path: adbg_axi_module -> AXI -> peripheral bus -> crypto core internal state exposed via CSR read or memory-mapped register.
obligations.uncertainty: Unclear if crypto exists; safe_domain might house it. Need to verify.

obligations.reason: The debug interface may be enabled regardless of security lifecycle state, allowing debug access in product deployment stages where it should be disabled.
obligations.subject: Debug interface enable logic
obligations.expected_guard: A secure lifecycle state signal (e.g., from OTP/fuses) must gate the debug clock or reset, preventing debug operation in secure or deployed states.
obligations.candidate_path: lifecycle_state -> debug enable generation; adbg_tap_top clock gating.
obligations.uncertainty: Lifecycle control may be in safe_domain.
